text:
In this unit we are exploring ratios and how they are used to solve problems. At the end of this investigation, ask yourself:

What do you understand about ratios and reasoning with ratios?

Students may have noticed

  • percents are part-to-whole ratio comparisons with the form x for every 100;
  • percents are useful for solving ratio problems because they are a convenient way to compare two ratios with different wholes;
  • diagrams (tape and area models) are helpful to visualize how large a percentage is;
  • a percent bar tells how much of a quantity is in 100% and can be used to determine how much is in 1%;
  • knowing how much 1% is can be used to find other percents by multiplying;
  • percents are used to compare values relative to a total amount (the whole); and
  • percents cannot be used to directly compare two quantities if they are not part of the same whole.
